tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Games with Friends.
Part 1 (interview): Do you often play games with your friends? / What kinds of games did you play when you were younger? / Do you prefer playing games indoors or outdoors?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a game you enjoy playing with your friends. You should say: what the game is, who you usually play it with, how the game is played, and explain why you enjoy playing it with friends rather than alone.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do you think games are an important part of friendships? / Do adults and children benefit from games in different ways? / Is competition in games generally a positive or negative thing for society?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——和朋友玩的游戏
referencePoints: Part 2: name a specific game (card game, board game, video game, or outdoor game) and set the scene with when and where you play it
Describe the rules briefly and the social atmosphere — laughter, teamwork, friendly rivalry
Explain the 'why' clearly: bonding, shared memories, relieving stress together
Part 3: discuss cooperation vs competition, social skills from games, and cultural attitudes to winning
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: An Older Person You Respect.
Part 1 (interview): Do you spend much time with older people in your family or community? / What can younger people learn from older generations? / Do you enjoy listening to older people's stories about the past?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe an older person you know and respect. You should say: who this person is, how you know them, what qualities they have, and explain why you respect them so much.
Part 3 (discussion): How has the role of elderly people in society changed over the past few decades? / Should governments do more to support older citizens, or is that the family's responsibility? / Do you think modern society values experience less than it used to?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——一位你尊敬的长者
referencePoints: Part 2: choose a concrete person (grandparent, elderly neighbour, retired teacher) and give a brief portrait
Mention two or three admirable qualities with a short example for each — patience, resilience, generosity
End with a personal reflection on what their example has taught you
Part 3: compare traditional extended families with modern living arrangements; balance state support against family duty
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: A Website That Helps You Learn.
Part 1 (interview): How often do you use the internet for studying? / Do you prefer learning from websites or from books? / What was the last thing you looked up online?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a website that helps you learn something. You should say: what the website is, how you found out about it, how often you use it, and explain how it has helped your learning.
Part 3 (discussion): Do you think online learning will ever completely replace classroom teaching? / How can people judge whether information on a website is reliable? / Should high-quality educational websites be free for everyone?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——一个帮助你学习的网站
referencePoints: Part 2: pick one specific site (a language platform, video tutorials, an encyclopedia-style site) and describe its main features
Give a concrete example of something you actually learned from it
Explain the benefit: convenience, self-paced study, free access, engaging format
Part 3: weigh online flexibility against classroom interaction; discuss misinformation and equal access to education
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Being Early or Late.
Part 1 (interview): Are you usually early, on time, or late for appointments? / How do you feel when other people keep you waiting? / Do you use anything to help you manage your time?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a time when you arrived very early or very late for something. You should say: what the occasion was, why you were early or late, what happened as a result, and explain how you felt about the experience.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do attitudes towards punctuality differ between cultures? / Has modern technology made people more or less punctual? / Is being constantly busy a sign of success or a problem in today's society?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——早到或迟到
referencePoints: Part 2: tell a clear story with a timeline — the event, the reason (traffic, misreading the time, over-caution), the consequence
Include how others reacted and what you did while waiting or after arriving late
Reflect on whether the experience changed your habits
Part 3: contrast strict-punctuality cultures with more flexible ones; discuss phones enabling last-minute changes and excuses
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: A Season You Enjoy.
Part 1 (interview): How many distinct seasons are there where you live? / Does your daily routine change with the seasons? / What clothes do you like wearing in cold weather?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a season of the year that you particularly enjoy. You should say: which season it is, what the weather is like then, what activities you do during this season, and explain why you enjoy it more than other seasons.
Part 3 (discussion): How do seasons affect people's moods and behaviour? / In what ways do seasonal changes influence a country's economy? / Do you think climate change is altering how people experience the seasons?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——你喜欢的季节
referencePoints: Part 2: use sensory description — temperature, light, colours, smells typical of the season
Link the season to specific activities: festivals, food, sports, family gatherings
Make a comparison with a season you like less to justify your preference
Part 3: mention seasonal mood changes, tourism and agriculture cycles, and shifting weather patterns
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Good Advice.
Part 1 (interview): Who do you usually ask for advice when you have a problem? / Do you find it easy to give advice to other people? / Have you ever ignored advice and regretted it?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a piece of good advice someone gave you. You should say: what the advice was, who gave it to you, why they gave you this advice, and explain how following it affected your life.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do some people find it difficult to accept advice? / Is advice from family members more valuable than advice from friends or professionals? / Do you think people today rely too much on the internet for advice?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——你收到的一条好建议
referencePoints: Part 2: quote or paraphrase the advice in one memorable sentence, then give the backstory
Describe the situation you were in and why the advice was exactly what you needed
Show the outcome with a before-and-after contrast
Part 3: discuss pride and independence as barriers to accepting advice; compare emotional support from family with expert professional guidance
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: Borrowing Books.
Part 1 (interview): Do you prefer borrowing books or buying your own copies? / Is there a library near where you live? / Have you ever lent a book to someone and never got it back?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a book you borrowed from a library or a friend. You should say: what the book was, who or where you borrowed it from, why you wanted to read it, and explain whether you would recommend it to others.
Part 3 (discussion): Do public libraries still matter in the digital age? / Why are some people reluctant to lend their belongings to others? / How might the sharing of resources, rather than owning them, change society in the future?
referencePoints: Part 2: identify the book's genre and give a spoiler-light summary of what it is about
Explain the borrowing context — a friend's recommendation, a library display, needing it for study
Give a clear verdict with reasons: writing style, usefulness, emotional impact
Part 3: cover libraries as community spaces, trust and ownership, and the wider sharing economy
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: An Interesting Building.
Part 1 (interview): What kinds of buildings are common in your hometown? / Do you prefer modern buildings or older, traditional ones? / Would you like to live in a very tall building?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe an interesting building you have seen or visited. You should say: where the building is, what it looks like, what it is used for, and explain why you find it interesting.
Part 3 (discussion): Should cities spend money preserving old buildings, or focus on new construction? / How does architecture affect the way people feel in a city? / Do you think buildings in the future will look very different from today's?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——一座有趣的建筑
referencePoints: Part 2: describe the exterior and interior with concrete details — materials, shape, size, unusual features
Mention its function and any history or story attached to it
Explain the personal angle: when you first saw it and the impression it made
Part 3: weigh heritage preservation costs against housing needs; discuss green buildings and smart architecture
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: A Small Daily Habit.
Part 1 (interview): What is the first thing you usually do in the morning? / Do you think you have more good habits or bad habits? / Is it easy for you to start doing something new every day?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a small habit that improves your day. You should say: what the habit is, when and how you started it, how often you do it, and explain how it makes your day better.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do people find it hard to keep good habits? / Do you think schools should teach children how to build healthy routines? / How much of a person's success depends on daily habits rather than talent?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——一个改善你一天的小习惯
referencePoints: Part 2: choose one small, concrete habit — a morning walk, journalling, tidying your desk, a short stretch
Tell the origin story: what prompted you to start and how you made it stick
Describe the tangible effect on mood, energy, or productivity
Part 3: discuss willpower vs environment design, habit education for children, and consistency versus natural ability
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2

tasks.prompt:
IELTS Speaking practice set — topic: A Journey That Didn't Go as Planned.
Part 1 (interview): How do you usually plan a trip before you leave? / What do you take with you on a long journey? / Do you get stressed when travel arrangements change suddenly?
Part 2 (long turn, 1 min prep, speak up to 2 min): Describe a journey that did not go as you had planned. You should say: where you were going, what went wrong during the journey, what you did to deal with the situation, and explain what you learned from the experience.
Part 3 (discussion): Why do some people enjoy unpredictable travel while others avoid it? / Has technology made travel problems easier or harder to deal with? / Do you think difficult experiences teach people more than comfortable ones?
tasks.promptZh: 雅思口语练习组——一次不按计划进行的旅程
referencePoints: Part 2: structure as a narrative — the plan, the disruption (missed connection, breakdown, sudden weather), the response
Include your feelings at each stage: frustration, worry, eventual relief or amusement
Finish with a genuine takeaway: flexibility, patience, better preparation
Part 3: contrast adventurous and cautious traveller personalities; discuss real-time travel apps and resilience built through adversity
tasks.wordLimit: speak up to 2 minutes in Part 2
